page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfc6.6xlarge is a compute-optimized virtual server instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with 24 dedicated physical cores and 24 vCPUs, utilizing an Intel Xeon 8269CY processor running at a clock speed of 3.1 GHz. The CPU configuration includes 32 KB L1d cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 35 MB L3 cache, with support for AVX, AVX512F, and AVX512_VNNI vector extensions. The instance is equipped with 48.0 GB of system memory, yielding a 2.0 GB per core ratio, and has a baseline network bandwidth of 8 Gbps. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware.

In benchmark evaluations, the instance demonstrates solid compute capabilities with a stress-ng single-core score of 1437.29 and a multi-core score of 23003.67. Memory performance is characterized by a read-only small block cached bandwidth of 1277.1 GB/sec and a large block uncached write bandwidth of 36.67 GB/sec. For specialized tasks, the server achieves a Geekbench compiling software score of 19083.0 and a ray tracing score of 18168.0. Database operations are supported by a Passmark database score of 8593.7 and a Redis SET throughput of 21.93 million operations per second. For lightweight machine learning, the system processes 135M parameter LLM prompts at 1275.25 tokens per second and 7B parameter prompts at 34.41 tokens per second.

The instance offers high compute density and dedicated CPU allocation, making it highly cost-efficient for CPU-bound workloads that do not require massive memory footprints. However, the lack of local storage requires external block storage attachment, which may introduce latency tradeoffs. The 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio also limits its suitability for memory-intensive databases. This profile is best suited for high-performance computing, software compilation, web serving, and moderate database operations.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfc6.6xlarge is a compute-optimized virtual server instance featuring 24 dedicated vCPUs and 24 physical cores on an Intel Xeon 8269CY processor running at 3.1 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture and KVM hypervisor, it provides 48.0 GB of RAM, a baseline network bandwidth of 8 Gbps, and no local storage. Benchmark results show a stress-ng multi-core CPU score of 23003.67 and a Redis SET throughput of 21.93 million operations per second. The instance is highly cost-efficient for compute-heavy workloads due to its dedicated CPU allocation and high clock speed, though the 2.0 GB per core memory ratio and lack of local storage present tradeoffs for memory-intensive or storage-heavy tasks. It is well-suited for software compilation, web serving, and moderate database operations.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfc6.6xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 24 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 48 GB RAM, and 8 Gbps network bandwidth, designed for high-performance computing and web serving.

tagline: A dedicated compute-optimized virtual server featuring high-frequency Intel Xeon processors and balanced memory for intensive CPU-bound workloads.
bullet_points: 24 dedicated Intel Xeon 8269CY vCPUs running at 3.1 GHz
48.0 GB of system memory with a 2.0 GB per core ratio
8 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage
Strong multi-core performance with a stress-ng score of 23003.67
Optimized for software compilation, web serving, and database operations
